title: ESC 2019 Guidelines on Acute Pulmonary Embolism: Diagnosis and Management
source: European Society of Cardiology (ESC) / European Respiratory Society (ERS)
description: Evidence-based guidelines for risk stratification, anticoagulation, reperfusion therapy, and long-term management of acute pulmonary embolism, including massive, submassive, and low-risk presentations.
recommendations: IMMEDIATE ANTICOAGULATION: Start anticoagulation without delay in high or intermediate clinical probability — do not wait for imaging confirmation
DIAGNOSTIC IMAGING: CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) is the gold standard for PE confirmation in hemodynamically stable patients
WELLS SCORE: Use Wells criteria to stratify pre-test probability; score >4 indicates high probability, proceed directly to CTPA
D-DIMER: PERC rule if low clinical probability; if PERC negative, no further workup needed; if positive, obtain age-adjusted D-dimer

MASSIVE PE (hemodynamically unstable): Systemic thrombolysis with alteplase 100mg IV over 2 hours is first-line; consider surgical embolectomy or catheter-directed thrombolysis if thrombolysis contraindicated
SUBMASSIVE PE (RV dysfunction, hemodynamically stable): Anticoagulation alone; escalate to reperfusion if clinical deterioration
LOW-RISK PE: DOAC monotherapy preferred — rivaroxaban 15mg twice daily x 21 days then 20mg daily, or apixaban 10mg twice daily x 7 days then 5mg twice daily
ANTICOAGULATION DURATION: Minimum 3 months for provoked PE; extended therapy (indefinite) for unprovoked PE or malignancy-associated PE
CANCER-ASSOCIATED PE: LMWH (dalteparin) or rivaroxaban preferred over warfarin for malignancy-associated VTE
INFERIOR VENA CAVA FILTER: Only when anticoagulation is absolutely contraindicated; retrieve when anticoagulation becomes feasible
ECHOCARDIOGRAPHY: Bedside TTE for hemodynamically unstable patients to assess RV function and exclude alternative diagnoses
TROPONIN AND BNP: Elevated troponin or BNP indicates RV strain — intermediate-high risk category; consider ICU monitoring
POST-PE FOLLOW-UP: Repeat echocardiography at 3-6 months; evaluate for chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) if persistent dyspnea
DVT WORKUP: Bilateral lower extremity compression ultrasound if CTPA non-diagnostic or radiation concern
